title: salix subtriandra
intro: Salix subtriandra, also known as the alder-leaved willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help to relieve pain and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Salix subtriandra include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Salix subtriandra can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its bark can also be dried and used in herbal teas, capsules, or topical creams.
